exercises.title: Trace a Command
exercises.description: Use strace to see the syscalls made by a simple command.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can identify the most frequent syscalls in the command.

exercises.title: Read the Current Syscall
exercises.description: Inspect the current process syscall information exposed by the kernel.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can see the active syscall slot and its arguments.

exercises.title: File I/O in C
exercises.description: Write a small program that opens, reads, writes, and closes a file using raw syscalls or wrappers.
exercises.expected_outcome: The program should create or modify a file and handle errors cleanly.

exercises.title: Syscall Timing
exercises.description: Measure the cost of repeatedly calling getpid() or another cheap syscall.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can compare syscall overhead with a regular function call.

exercises.title: Tiny Shell Pipeline
exercises.description: Implement a mini shell feature that spawns a child process, redirects stdout, and handles exit codes.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can observe correct process and file descriptor behavior.

exercises.title: Seccomp Filter
exercises.description: Add a restrictive seccomp filter to a test program and verify blocked syscalls fail.
exercises.expected_outcome: Disallowed syscalls should be denied while the allowed path still runs.
